sections.content: Let me check where the purple square moved to after clicking (17, 46).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check purple square position
after = current_frame.segmentation
purple_after = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
print("Purple objects:")
for n in purple_after:
    print(f"  boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

# Check gray dots
gray_after = [n for n in after['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 1]
print(f"\nGray single pixels: {len(gray_after)}")
for n in sorted(gray_after, key=lambda x: x['boundary'][0][0]):
    print(f"  {n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The purple square didn't move! It's still at [[50, 11], [50, 13], [52, 13], [52, 11]]. The gray dot at (17, 46) is still there. So clicking on a gray dot that's far away doesn't work - it only moves to adjacent gray dots along the path.
Wait, but the first click moved it from the bottom-left to the white cross position, which was several dots away. Let me reconsider...
Actually, looking back:
- Initial: purple at (58-60, 3-5), white cross at (52-54, 9-11)
- After first click (on white cross): purple moved to (52-54, 9-11)
- After second click (on blue character at 15, 48): purple didn't move
- After third click (on gray dot at 51, 12): purple moved to (50-52, 11-13)
- After fourth click (on gray dot at 17, 46): purple didn't move
So it seems like:
1. Clicking on the white cross moved the purple square there (the cross was adjacent to the purple square's starting position)
2. Clicking on the blue character didn't work (too far away)
3. Clicking on the adjacent gray dot (51, 12) moved the purple square there
4. Clicking on a far gray dot (17, 46) didn't work
So the mechanic is: clicking on an adjacent gray dot (or special target like the white cross) moves the purple square one step along the path.
The purple square is currently at approximately (51, 12). The next gray dot along the path toward the blue character would be at (49, 14). Let me click on that.
Actually wait - let me reconsider. The first move was from (58-60, 3-5) to (52-54, 9-11). That's a jump of about 6 rows and 6 columns. The white cross was at (52-54, 9-11), which is where the purple square moved to. So clicking on the white cross moved the purple square there, even though it was several dots away.
But clicking on (17, 46) didn't work. So maybe the rule is:
- You can only click on positions that are adjacent to the current purple square position
- The white cross was adjacent to the purple square's starting position
Let me check: the purple square was at (58-60, 3-5), and the white cross was at (52-54, 9-11). The gray dots between them were at (55, 8) and (57, 6). So the white cross was 2-3 dots away, not adjacent.
Hmm, maybe the white cross was a special "teleport" target? Or maybe the rule is different.
Let me look at the current state more carefully. The purple square is at (50-52, 11-13). The gray dots above it are at (49, 14), (47, 16), etc. The gray dots below it are at (53, 10), (55, 8), (57, 6).
Wait, I just realized something. When I clicked on (51, 12), the purple square moved to (50-52, 11-13). But (51, 12) was a gray dot position. The purple square is 3x3, so its center would be at (51, 12). So clicking on the gray dot moved the purple square to that position.
When I clicked on (17, 46), the purple square didn't move. This suggests that the click position must be adjacent to the current purple square position.
Let me check: the purple square is at (50-52, 11-13), so its edges are at rows 50-52 and cols 11-13. The gray dot at (49, 14) is at row 49, col 14. Is this adjacent to the purple square? Row 49 is adjacent to row 50 (the top edge of the purple square), and col 14 is adjacent to col 13 (the right edge). So yes, (49, 14) is diagonally adjacent to the purple square.
But (17, 46) is far away from (50-52, 11-13), so it's not adjacent.
So the rule is: clicking on an adjacent gray dot moves the purple square to that position.
Now, to efficiently move the purple square along the path, I need to click on each adjacent gray dot in sequence. The path goes:
(49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32), (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42), (19, 44), (17, 46)
That's 17 more clicks to reach the gray dot at (17, 46), which is adjacent to the blue character.
But wait - the efficiency budget says "4 of about 22 target actions". If I need 17 more clicks, that's 21 total, which is close to 22. So this seems right.
Let me batch the remaining clicks to move the purple square all the way to the blue character. I'll click on each gray dot in sequence.
Actually, I need to be careful. Each click moves the purple square one step, and the next click needs to be on the next adjacent gray dot. Let me create the sequence of clicks.
The path from the current purple square position (50-52, 11-13) going up toward the blue character:
1. (49, 14)
2. (47, 16)
3. (45, 18)
4. (43, 20)
5. (41, 22)
6. (39, 24)
7. (37, 26)
8. (35, 28)
9. (33, 30)
10. (31, 32)
11. (29, 34)
12. (27, 36)
13. (25, 38)
14. (23, 40)
15. (21, 42)
16. (19, 44)
17. (17, 46)
After reaching (17, 46), the purple square will be adjacent to the blue character. Maybe I need to click on the blue character to complete the level?
Let me batch all these clicks.

# Path: (49,14), (47,16), (45,18), (43,20), (41,22), (39,24), (37,26), (35,28), (33,30), (31,32), (29,34), (27,36), (25,38), (23,40), (21,42), (19,44), (17,46)

# Build the sequence of MOUSE actions
